perturbation.original_text: Franchisee or third party sub -franchisees shall be under an obligation to set up at least 6 (six) Smaaash Centres during the first Contract Year or any other time period as may be provided by Franchisor. The Smaaash Centres shall be established and operated in the Territory using the assumed trade name 'Smaaash'or any other trade name that the Franchisor may designate. Franchisee shall maintain good customer relations in accordance with prudent and reasonable business practices.
perturbation.changed_text: Franchisee or third party sub -franchisees should endeavor to set up approximately 6 (six) Smaaash Centres during the first Contract Year or any other time period as may be provided by Franchisor. The Smaaash Centres shall be established and operated in the Territory using the assumed trade name 'Smaaash'or any other trade name that the Franchisor may designate. Franchisee shall maintain good customer relations in accordance with prudent and reasonable business practices.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'shall be under an obligation to set up at least 6' to 'should endeavor to set up approximately 6' introduces ambiguity. The original text clearly mandates the establishment of six Smaaash Centres, making it a contractual obligation. The revised text weakens this requirement, suggesting it is merely a target rather than a binding commitment. This contradicts the entire agreement, that holds the Franchisee accountable to reach the standards of the Franchisor. Thus creates an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to Section 1.2, Franchisor hereby grants to Franchisee the exclusive right, (a) to establish and operate Smaaash Centres in the Territory, (b) to sub-license the right to establish and operate Smaaash Centres to third party franchisees in and for the Territory, (c) a license to use the products and other services developed by Franchisor with respect to the Smaaash Centres (including a right to authorise the use of products and services developed by the Franchisor by third party franchisees), in the Territory, and (d) to identify third party franchisees for the Smaaash Centres in the Territory. The rights granted herein include the limited license to use the Trademarks of the Franchisor (the details of which are morefully set out in Exhibit A), as set out in Section 3 of this Agreement, for the purposes of establishing and operating the Smaaash Centres in the Territory. It is clarified that if third party franchisees shall be establishing and operating Smaaash Centres in the manner contemplated under this Agreement, then the Franchisee shall ensure that such third party franchisees complies with all the obligations and duties of the Franchisee, as recorded under this Agreement, and to this extent, Franchisee may enter into relevant agreements with such third party franchisees.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the establishment of at least 6 Smaaash Centres ('shall be under an obligation'), creating a clear contractual duty. The changed text weakens this requirement to 'should endeavor to set up approximately 6', which introduces ambiguity and suggests the establishment of 6 centres is merely a target, not a binding obligation. This contradicts the contradicted text, which grants the Franchisee rights 'to establish and operate Smaaash Centres in the Territory'. By weakening the obligation to establish a specific number of centres, the changed text undermines the basis of the granted rights, creating a direct and impactful conflict and thus, an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Franchisee shall comply with, and ensure that the third party sub-franchisees comply with the standards prescribed by Franchisor (as provided in the operating manuals which shall be shared by Franchisor with Franchisee) with respect to the services, products and operations of the Smaaash Centres and shall operate the Smaaash Centres in strict conformity with such standards and specifications as Franchisor may from time to time prescribe to Franchisee. Franchisee shall refrain from deviating from such standards and specifications without Franchisor's prior written consent and from otherwise operating in any manner which reflects adversely on the Trademarks and Smaaash Centres.
perturbation.changed_text: Franchisee shall endeavor to comply with, and ensure that the third party sub-franchisees endeavor to comply with the standards prescribed by Franchisor (as provided in the operating manuals which shall be shared by Franchisor with Franchisee) with respect to the services, products and operations of the Smaaash Centres and shall operate the Smaaash Centres in reasonable conformity with such standards and specifications as Franchisor may from time to time prescribe to Franchisee. Franchisee shall only refrain from deviating from such standards and specifications if Franchisor objects and from otherwise operating in any manner which reflects adversely on the Trademarks and Smaaash Centres.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates compliance with the standards. Changing 'shall comply' to 'shall endeavor to comply' and 'strict conformity' to 'reasonable conformity' weakens the obligation, introducing ambiguity about what constitutes acceptable compliance. This creates contradiction against the clause in the Agreement. Thus creates an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to Section 1.2, Franchisor hereby grants to Franchisee the exclusive right, (a) to establish and operate Smaaash Centres in the Territory, (b) to sub-license the right to establish and operate Smaaash Centres to third party franchisees in and for the Territory, (c) a license to use the products and other services developed by Franchisor with respect to the Smaaash Centres (including a right to authorise the use of products and services developed by the Franchisor by third party franchisees), in the Territory, and (d) to identify third party franchisees for the Smaaash Centres in the Territory. The rights granted herein include the limited license to use the Trademarks of the Franchisor (the details of which are morefully set out in Exhibit A), as set out in Section 3 of this Agreement, for the purposes of establishing and operating the Smaaash Centres in the Territory. It is clarified that if third party franchisees shall be establishing and operating Smaaash Centres in the manner contemplated under this Agreement, then the Franchisee shall ensure that such third party franchisees complies with all the obligations and duties of the Franchisee, as recorded under this Agreement, and to this extent, Franchisee may enter into relevant agreements with such third party franchisees.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates strict compliance by the franchisee and sub-franchisees with the franchisor's standards. The changed text weakens this obligation by using 'endeavor to comply' and 'reasonable conformity,' thus introducing ambiguity regarding the level of compliance required. This contradicts the original agreement clause, which requires the franchisee to ensure that sub-franchisees comply with all obligations and duties of the franchisee, as recorded under the agreement. The original text creates a strong standard that franchisees must follow while the changed text softens that making it difficult for the Franchisor to hold the franchisee or sub-franchisees liable. This contradiction falls under Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Franchisee shall indemnify and hold the Franchisor, its affiliates and their respective agents and employees harmless from all claims, actions, suits, damages, costs and expenses in relation to or arising out of the breach of any representations, warranties, covenants and obligations of the Franchisee as set out in this Agreement. The indemnification rights of the Franchisor shall be without prejudice to, and independent of any other rights and remedies that the Franchisor may have at law or in equity, including the right to seek specific performance, injunctive relief or restitution, none of which rights or remedies shall be affected or diminished thereby.
perturbation.changed_text: The Franchisee should aim to indemnify and hold the Franchisor, its affiliates and their respective agents and employees harmless from significant claims, actions, suits, damages, costs and expenses directly related to or clearly arising out of the significant breach of essential representations, warranties, covenants and obligations of the Franchisee as explicitly set out in this Agreement. The indemnification rights of the Franchisor shall be subject to and dependent on any other rights and remedies that the Franchisor may have at law or in equity, including the right to seek specific performance, injunctive relief or restitution, all of which rights or remedies shall be affected or diminished thereby.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'shall indemnify' to 'should aim to indemnify' weakens the obligation. Limiting indemnity to 'significant claims' and 'essential representations' reduces the scope of protection. Making indemnification rights 'dependent on' other remedies changes the nature of the protection offered to Franchisor. This creates a contradiction with the rest of the text of the Agreement. Thus creates an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the parties represents and warrants to the other party that, (i) the Agreement constitutes a valid, legal and binding obligation of such party and is enforceable against such party in accordance with its terms, (ii) it has the power and authority to execute the Agreement and perform all its terms, and (iii) the execution and performance of this Agreement shall not violate any charter documents of such party, contravene any provisions of law as applicable to such party (including any order, decree, injunction of any competent court) or conflict with the provisions of any material agreement or contract executed by such party. The provisions of this Section 10.4 shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text imposes a strict obligation on the Franchisee to indemnify the Franchisor. The changed text weakens this obligation by using "should aim to indemnify" instead of "shall indemnify," limiting indemnity to "significant claims" and "essential representations," and making indemnification rights "dependent on" other remedies. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that the Agreement constitutes a valid, legal, and binding obligation enforceable against the parties in accordance with its terms. The change introduces ambiguity and reduces the enforceability of the indemnification clause, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction making the two sections incompatible. This alteration also aligns with the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition, where obligations are defined inconsistently. The initial obligation to Indemnify becomes less certain, which effects the validity and legal binding of the original agreement as stated in the 'Contradicted Text'.
